Turn Rates & Days Supply By Carline & Trim
Monthly Turn Rate % by Carline
Turn Rate % by Model Year & Trim
Avg Days on Lot by Model Year & Trim
Detailed Turn Rate & Days Supply
Definitions

Turn Rate % — Units sold as a share of total available inventory: sales / (sales + current OG) x 100. Represents what percentage of the starting inventory position moved in the period.

MTD Turn Rate % — Current month sales prorated to a full month, then: prorated sales / (prorated sales + OG) x 100.

Prior Month Turn Rate % — Prior month actual sales / (prior month sales + current OG) x 100.

R90 Turn Rate % — Average monthly sales over trailing 90 days / (avg monthly sales + current OG) x 100. The most stable velocity measure.

Days on Lot (DOL) — Average number of days current on-ground units have been in "Dealer Stock" status, sourced from the Master File "Days In Stock" field. Lower means fresher inventory. Color-coded: green 45d or less, grey 46-90d, red over 90d.

Scope — MY25 and MY26 only. Excludes MY24, fleet, internal, and unassigned stock. US and Canada retail network only.

Customer Experience Scorecard Review Ratings Across Platforms
Setup: Auto-Fetch Review Ratings

To automatically populate ratings, set these environment variables before running the refresh script:

set GOOGLE_PLACES_KEY=your_google_api_key
set YELP_API_KEY=your_yelp_api_key
python dashboard_refresh_all_in_one.py

Google Places API — Free $200/mo credit (~10,000 lookups). Enable here ↗

Yelp Fusion API — Free tier: 5,000 calls/mo. Get key here ↗

Meanwhile, use the ↗ links in the table below to manually check each dealer's reviews on any platform.

Top 10 Dealers by Composite Score
Bottom 10 Dealers by Composite Score
Google Yelp DealerRater Cars.com Composite
Dealer RatingReviews RatingReviews RatingReviews RatingReviews ScoreTotal Reviews
How It Works

Composite Score — Weighted average of available platform ratings: Google (35%), Yelp (25%), DealerRater (25%), Cars.com (15%). Only platforms with data are included in the calculation.

Data Sources — Google Places API, Yelp Fusion API, DealerRater (web), Cars.com (web). Data refreshes every 24 hours when the dashboard script is run.

Platform Links — Click the arrow icons to open the dealer's page on each platform in a new tab.

Sales Density Map
Average Customer Distance from Dealership
Cross-Sell Analysis Conquest vs Own-Market Sales
Dealer Conquest Scorecard
Top Conquest Flows
Conquest Flow Diagram All Dealer-to-Dealer Flows
Methodology

Each sale is assigned to its nearest dealer based on the Haversine distance between the customer's geocoded address and every dealership location. If the selling dealer matches the nearest dealer, it is classified as an own-market sale. If a different dealer was closer, it is a conquest sale for the seller and a lost sale for the nearest dealer.

Net Conquest = Conquest sales won minus sales lost to other dealers. Positive means the dealer is a net exporter of demand (pulling customers from other markets). Negative means other dealers are pulling customers from their natural territory.

Scope: Includes only geocoded sold units (US + Canada). Sales from internal stock accounts and units without customer coordinates are excluded. Nearest-dealer assignment is purely geographic and does not account for customer preference, referrals, or inventory availability.

Lead Quality Analysis Brand Leads Only | Source: Raw Lead Data
Quality Metrics Over Time (Monthly)
Missing Phone % (Monthly)
Low-Intent Lead % (Monthly)
Top Repeat Lead Submitters (3+ leads from same email)
Email (masked)Lead CountRetailer(s)
Retailer Lead Quality Scorecard (Rolling 180 Days, min 20 leads)
RetailerRegionLeadsRepeat %No Phone %Same-Mo Repeat %Low Intent %Bad/DupBad Contact %Quality Score
Definitions

Repeat % — Percentage of leads submitted from an email address that has appeared more than once across the entire lead database. Flags customers who have submitted multiple inquiries over time, which may indicate recycled or stale interest.

No Phone % — Percentage of leads with no landline or mobile phone number on file. These leads can only be contacted by email, significantly reducing the probability of engagement. A rising trend may indicate a form or source issue.

Same-Mo Repeat % — Percentage of leads from an email address that submitted more than once within the same calendar month. This is a stronger signal of form spam, accidental resubmissions, or bot activity than cross-month repeats, which may reflect legitimate renewed interest.

Low Intent % — Percentage of leads categorized as "Dealer contact request" or "Quotation request" rather than "Test drive request." Test drive requests indicate active purchase consideration and higher buyer intent. Lower-intent categories may still convert but at significantly lower rates, and a rising share signals a shift in the quality of inbound demand.

Bad/Dup — Count of leads whose CRM status field contains keywords such as "bad lead," "duplicate," "fake," "bogus," "test lead," or "not qualified." These represent leads that should not count toward actionable volume.

Bad Contact % — Percentage of leads where the CRM status field indicates invalid or unusable contact information: wrong numbers, disconnected lines, bad email addresses, or no valid contact details. These leads were unworkable regardless of dealer effort.

Quality Score — Composite score from 0 to 100. Starts at 100 and deducts weighted penalties: Repeat % (x0.25), No Phone % (x0.20), Same-Mo Repeat % (x0.20), Low Intent % (x0.15), Bad/Dup rate (x0.10), Bad Contact % (x0.10). Higher is better. Scores below 50 are flagged red; 50 to 69 amber; 70+ green.

R180 — Rolling 180-day window. All retailer-level metrics are calculated over the most recent 180 days to balance recency with sample size.

Scope — All metrics are filtered to brand leads only (Source = "Web site"). Non-brand leads, event leads, and dealer-generated leads are excluded.

Dealer Scorecard
Scoring Methodology

Composite Score (0-100) — Weighted blend of six performance categories. Each sub-metric is scored 0-100 using absolute benchmarks, then rolled up into a category score, then weighted into the composite.

Sales Velocity (25%)

Turn Rate (50%) — Prior month turn rate, weighted avg of SW + QM

MTD Sales (30%) — Current month units, peer-normalized

MoM Momentum (20%) — MTD vs prior month ratio

Lead Management (20%)

Contact Pending % (30%) — Lower = better lead follow-up

Lead-to-TD % (40%) — R3M test drive bookings / leads

TD Show Rate (30%) — Completed TDs / booked TDs

Conversion (20%)

Lead-to-Sale % (40%) — All-time brand leads converted to sales

R90 Matchback % (35%) — 90-day lead-to-sale attribution

W/S:H/O Gap (25%) — Wholesale-to-handover ratio, 1:1 = ideal

Inventory Health (15%)

Avg Days on Lot (35%) — Weighted avg DOL of OG stock, lower = better

Turn Rate (30%) — Same as velocity, rewards efficient inventory

Model Mix (20%) — Stocking all 3 models (SW + QM + SVO) = full score

Trim Mix (20%) — Distinct trims in stock out of 6 (Base, Fieldmaster, Trialmaster, Black Edition, Arcane Works, Belstaff)

Trend (10%)

H/O Trend (60%) — R3M handover change vs prior R3M

Lead Trend (40%) — R3M lead volume change vs prior R3M

Engagement (10%)

TD Volume R3M (60%) — Test drive bookings, peer-normalized

TD Events (40%) — Weekend + program TD bookings combined

Sources: Dealer Performance Dashboard, Dealer Inventory Report, Lead Handling KPIs

Pipeline Aging
Pipeline Waterfall
MY25 vs MY26 by Stage
StageUnits% of TotalMY25MY26Avg Days in StockDistribution
10 Oldest Units in Dealer Stock
RankDealerMarketVINModelTrimExt ColorDays in Stock
Definitions

Awaiting Status — Units with blank status in the Master File. Typically pre-order or awaiting system assignment.

Preplanning — Order placed, not yet in production queue.

Production — Actively being assembled at Hambach plant.

Built — Assembly complete, awaiting departure from plant.

On Water — Departed Hambach, in ocean transit to Americas port.

At Port — Arrived at US port of entry. Awaiting customs, PDI, or dispatch.

In-Transit — Dispatched from port, en route to dealership. Includes Planned for Transfer.

Dealer Stock — On ground at retailer. Available for retail sale.

Avg Days in Stock — From the Master File Days In Stock field. Only meaningful for Dealer Stock and In-Transit stages where units have been received into the SAP system.

Scope: All Grenadier units (SW + QM) across all markets, model years, and channels. Excludes Sold and Written Off. Source: Master File V14 — Export sheet, status column.

MY25 / MY26 Migration Tracker
Monthly Sales by Model Year
Dealer MY25 Aging Inventory

Dealers with MY25 on ground, sorted by MY25 OG count descending. Avg DOL for MY25 stock shown. Dealers with MY25 avg DOL over 120 days flagged.

DealerMarketMY25 OGMY25 Avg DOLMY26 OGMY25 R90 SalesMY26 R90 SalesMY25 Velocity
Key

MY25 OG — Count of MY25 units currently in Dealer Stock status. MY26 OG — Count of MY26 units currently in Dealer Stock status.

MY25 Avg DOL — Average days on lot for MY25 on-ground units. Dealers exceeding 120 days are flagged in red, indicating aged stock requiring attention.

R90 Sales — Retail handovers in the rolling 90-day window, split by model year. Used to gauge current demand velocity for each MY.

MY25 Velocity — Monthly sell-through rate for MY25 stock, calculated as MY25 R90 Sales / 3. Higher velocity means the dealer is moving MY25 units faster.

Crossover Month — The first month where MY26 retail sales exceed MY25 retail sales, signaling the network has shifted to the new model year as the primary selling unit.

Scope: US + Canada retail sales only. Excludes fleet, internal, CVP, and MY24 units. Monthly chart shows handover date by model year with CVP units excluded.

Trim Mix Optimization
Demand (R180 Sales) vs Supply (OG + Pipeline)
SpecR180 SalesDemand %Supply UnitsSupply %Gap (pp)Demand vs Supply
Key Findings
Definitions

Demand % — Share of R180 retail sales for each spec. Represents what customers are buying.

Supply % — Share of all non-sold pipeline and on-ground inventory for each spec. Represents what is available or incoming.

Gap (pp) — Supply % minus Demand %, in percentage points. Positive = oversupplied (more in pipeline than demand warrants). Negative = undersupplied (strong demand outpacing supply). Gaps beyond +/- 5pp flagged.

Dimensions — Trim Level, Exterior Color, Interior Color (leather black, grey/black, utility), Contrast Roof (none, black, white), Ladder Frame (black, halo red, rhino grey), Rough Pack (standard vs rough pack), Body Style (SW vs QM). Each analyzed independently.

Scope: US and Canada retail units. Demand = R180 sold units. Supply = all non-sold, non-written-off units. Source: Master File V14 — Export sheet.

Website Analytics Source: Google Analytics (GA4) | Property: Grenadier Website | ineosauto

How many people visit ineosgrenadier.com and through which channels? Organic users find us through search engines. Paid users arrive via advertising (Google Ads, social). Direct users type the URL or use bookmarks. The 7-day moving average smooths daily volatility. Current month bar shows a dashed projection to full-month pace.

Daily Users 7-Day Moving Average
Monthly Users
Channel Mix Monthly Stacked
Session Sources Full Period
MonthTotal UsersOrganicPaidDirectDaysTrend
Engagement Session Depth & Time on Site

How deeply do visitors interact with the site? Engagement time measures average seconds of active interaction per session (scrolling, clicking, reading), not just time with the tab open. Higher values indicate visitors are consuming content rather than bouncing. Organic users consistently engage longer than Paid, reflecting higher intent from search-driven discovery.

Daily Engagement Time 7-Day MA (seconds)
Monthly Engagement by Channel Avg seconds/session
Audience Profile Demographics, Interests & Language

Who is visiting? Demographics are based on Google's inferred user attributes where identifiable (roughly 20% of total traffic). Interests reflect Google's affinity categories, showing what else our audience cares about. These inform creative targeting and media planning decisions. Note: gender and age data only covers users signed into Google services.

Gender
Age Distribution
Top Audience Interests
Language
Geography Country & City Performance

Where are visitors located? Country and city data help identify which markets drive the most traffic and, more importantly, which convert at the highest rate. Key Event Rate (KER) measures the percentage of users who complete high-intent actions: test drive bookings, dealer locator searches, and configuration completions. A market with fewer users but higher KER may warrant increased investment. The bubble chart maps engagement depth against conversion rate to identify high-value markets.

Top Countries Active Users
Top Cities

Engagement vs Conversion Bubble = Volume (min 5K users)
CountryActive UsersNew UsersEng. RateAvg Eng. TimeKey EventsKey Evt RateQuality
Behavioral Segments GA4 Cohorts & Funnel

How do visitors progress through the purchase journey? GA4 tracks behavioral cohorts from first touch to ownership. Inspire and Curious are awareness-stage visitors. Explore, StoryStream, and Configurator indicate active consideration. Investigator, Leads, Build, and Buy signal purchase intent. Views/Session and Avg Duration increase dramatically as users move down-funnel, reflecting deeper engagement with pricing, specs, and dealer information.

Audience Funnel Users by Segment (log scale)
Session Depth Views/Session + Duration
Segments by Channel
AudienceUsersNewSessionsViews/SessAvg DurationDepth
Technology Device, Browser & OS

How do visitors access the site? Device and browser mix informs UX priorities and QA testing focus. Mobile dominance (driven by social ad traffic) means the mobile configurator and dealer locator experience are critical. Safari in-app represents users clicking through from social media apps (Instagram, Facebook) rather than the native browser, often with different rendering behavior. Screen resolutions are color-coded: red = mobile, black = desktop, blue = tablet.

Device Category
Device by Channel
Operating System
Browser
OS by Channel
Top Screen Resolutions Red=Mobile Black=Desktop Blue=Tablet
Paid Campaigns Google Ads Sessions | Full Period

Which paid campaigns drive site traffic? Filter by Market to compare regional campaign performance, or by Type to evaluate strategy mix. PMax uses automated Google placements. DemandGen targets mid-funnel comparison audiences. Brand Search captures users already searching for INEOS/Grenadier. Session data covers the full GA4 period (Jan 2025 to present).

Campaigns by Sessions
Sessions by Campaign Type
Glossary

Active Users — Unique visitors who initiated a session within the selected period.

New Users — First-time visitors with no prior session history in the GA4 property.

Sessions — Total visits. One user may generate multiple sessions across devices or return visits.

Engaged Session — A session lasting 10+ seconds, with 2+ page views, or containing a key event.

Engagement Rate — Percentage of sessions classified as engaged. Higher rates indicate relevant, quality traffic.

Engagement Time — Average seconds of active interaction per session (scrolling, clicking, reading). Excludes idle/background time.

Views/Session — Average page views per session. Higher values indicate deeper content exploration.

Key Events — High-intent user actions: test drive bookings, dealer locator searches, configuration completions, contact form submissions.

Key Event Rate (KER) — Key events as a percentage of active users. The primary website conversion metric.

7-Day Moving Average — Each point is the average of that day and the prior 6 days. Smooths daily volatility (weekends, holidays) to reveal underlying trends.

Rolling 3 Months — From the 1st of the month two months prior to current. Gives three calendar months of context with MTD included.

Projected — Current partial month extrapolated to full month: (MTD users / days elapsed) x days in month.

Channels

Organic — Unpaid search engine results (Google, Bing). Indicates brand awareness and SEO effectiveness.

Paid — Paid advertising (Google Ads, social). Performance depends on campaign targeting and creative quality.

Direct — Users typing the URL, using bookmarks, or clicking untracked links. Often indicates brand loyalty or offline-to-online conversion.

All Users — Aggregate across all channels including Email, Referral, Social, and others not broken out individually.

Behavioral Cohorts (Funnel Stages)

Awareness: Inspire, Curious, Curious USA, Curious v2 — first-touch and early-browse visitors.

Consideration: Explore, StoryStream, Configurator — visitors engaging with product content, building configurations, or interacting with lifestyle media.

Intent: Investigator, Leads, Build, Buy, Comparer — visitors requesting information, submitting leads, or comparing trims/specs.

Conversion: Decider, Owner, Advocate — purchase-stage visitors, current owners, and repeat/referral visitors.

Incomplete Reservation — Users who started but did not complete the reservation process. High session duration here indicates friction.

Source: Google Analytics (GA4) — Grenadier Website property. Account: ineosauto. Period: Jan 2025 to present. Demographics and interests data covers Google-identifiable users only (~20% of total traffic).
